Implement the following plan:

# Improve ExploreScreen to match webapp

## Context
The mobile ExploreScreen is currently a bare search-only page — it shows nothing until the user types 2+ characters, then displays simple text rows for results. The webapp's Explore page is much richer: it displays a **#1 Hottest post hero card**, **runners-up (#2–#5)**, and the same search functionality. This plan brings the mobile Explore page in line with the webapp.

## What changes

### Default view (before searching)
Instead of an empty state, show **monthly hottest posts**:
1. **Hero card** — The #1 hottest post as a full-width `PostCard` with a "#1 Hottest" badge (FireIcon + text overlay on top-left)
2. **Runners-up list** — Posts #2–#5 as compact ranked rows (rank number, title, author avatar+name, hot score pill)

### Search mode (when user types 2+ chars)
Keep the existing search behavior (users + posts tabs) but upgrade post results to use `PostCard` instead of plain text rows.

### New API method
Add `getHottestMonthly()` to `mobileApi.ts` — calls the existing server endpoint `GET /journals/hottest-monthly`.

### New utility
Add `calculateReadingTime()` to `journalHelpers.ts` — matches the webapp logic (150 wpm, returns "X min read").

## Files to edit

### 1. `apps/mobile/src/lib/api/mobileApi.ts`
- Add method:
  ```ts
  async getHottestMonthly(limit = 5, userId?: string): Promise<CursorPage<JournalItem>>
  ```
  Calls `GET /journals/hottest-monthly?limit={limit}&userId={userId}`

### 2. `apps/mobile/src/lib/utils/journalHelpers.ts`
- Add function:
  ```ts
  export const calculateReadingTime = (text: string): string => { ... }
  ```
  Logic: word count / 150 wpm, ceil, return `"X min read"` / `"X mins read"`

### 3. `apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/ExploreScreen.tsx` — full rewrite
**Imports to add:** `useAuth`, `PostCard`, `PostCardSkeleton`, `FireIcon`, `BadgeCheckIcon`, `mobileApi` methods, `extractPlainText`, `extractBannerImage`, `resolveCount`, `calculateReadingTime`, `useBookmarkMutation`, `useMutation`, `socialApi`, `queryClient`, `Image`

**New data fetching:**
- `hottestQuery` — `useQuery` calling `mobileApi.getHottestMonthly(5, user?.id)`, staleTime 5 min
- Keep existing `usersQuery` and `journalsQuery` for search

**New state:**
- Existing: `query`, `activeTab`
- Derive: `isSearching = normalizedQuery.length >= 2`

**Layout — two modes:**

**A) Default mode (`!isSearching`)**
```
SafeAreaView
  Header ("Explore" + SearchInput)
  ScrollView
    Hero section
      PostCard for #1 post with "#1 Hottest" badge overlay (FireIcon + label)
    Runners section
      SectionTitle "Top This Month"
      RunnerRow × 4 (#2–#5): rank | thumbnail | title + author | hot score pill
    Loading: 3x PostCardSkeleton
    Empty: EmptyState "No hot posts yet this month"
```

**B) Search mode (`isSearching`)**
```
SafeAreaView
  Header ("Explore" + SearchInput)
  Tab chips (All / Users / Posts)
  FlatList
    Users section (same as current, with Avatar rows)
    Posts section — upgrade from text rows to PostCard components
    Empty state
```

**Runner row component** (inline, not a separate file):
- `View` row: rank number (styled in amber for #2–3), post thumbnail (60×60 rounded), title (2 lines), author name, hot score pill with FireIcon
- Pressable → navigates to PostDetail

**Hero badge overlay:**
- Absolute-positioned View on top-left of PostCard
- FireIcon + "#1 Hottest" text
- Semi-transparent dark background with amber border, rounded

**Interaction handlers:**
- Reactions, bookmarks, comments, reposts — same pattern as HomeFeedScreen
- Author press → VisitProfile or Profile tab

## Reusable pieces (no new files needed)
- `PostCard` — already supports `bannerImage`, `readingTime`, `postType`, `bannerContent`
- `PostCardSkeleton` — loading state
- `FireIcon` — already exported from icons
- `Avatar`, `Chip`, `SearchInput`, `EmptyState` — already imported
- `extractPlainText`, `extractBannerImage`, `resolveCount` — from journalHelpers
- `useBookmarkMutation` — from hooks
- Server endpoint `GET /journals/hottest-monthly` — already exists

## Files NOT changed
- No new component files — everything is inline in ExploreScreen or uses existing components
- No navigation changes — ExploreScreen route already exists
- No server changes — endpoint already exists

## Verification
- ExploreScreen shows hottest posts on load (hero + runners)
- Tapping a hottest post navigates to PostDetail
- Typing 2+ chars switches to search mode with tab filters
- Search results show PostCard for posts, Avatar rows for users
- Clearing search returns to hottest view
- Loading skeletons display while hottest data loads
- Empty state shows when no hottest posts exist
- Reactions/bookmarks work on explore PostCards
- No new TypeScript errors

## Context
The mobile ExploreScreen is currently a bare search-only page — it shows nothing until the user types 2+ characters, then displays simple text rows for results. The webapp's Explore page is much richer: it displays a **#1 Hottest post hero card**, **runners-up (#2–#5)**, and the same search functionality. This plan brings the mobile Explore page in line with the webapp.

## What changes

### Default view (before searching)
Instead of an empty state, show **monthly hottest posts**:
1. **Hero card** — The #1 hottest post as a full-width `PostCard` with a "#1 Hottest" badge (FireIcon + text overlay on top-left)
2. **Runners-up list** — Posts #2–#5 as compact ranked rows (rank number, title, author avatar+name, hot score pill)

### Search mode (when user types 2+ chars)
Keep the existing search behavior (users + posts tabs) but upgrade post results to use `PostCard` instead of plain text rows.

### New API method
Add `getHottestMonthly()` to `mobileApi.ts` — calls the existing server endpoint `GET /journals/hottest-monthly`.

### New utility
Add `calculateReadingTime()` to `journalHelpers.ts` — matches the webapp logic (150 wpm, returns "X min read").

## Files to edit

### 1. `apps/mobile/src/lib/api/mobileApi.ts`
- Add method:
  ```ts
  async getHottestMonthly(limit = 5, userId?: string): Promise<CursorPage<JournalItem>>
  ```
  Calls `GET /journals/hottest-monthly?limit={limit}&userId={userId}`

### 2. `apps/mobile/src/lib/utils/journalHelpers.ts`
- Add function:
  ```ts
  export const calculateReadingTime = (text: string): string => { ... }
  ```
  Logic: word count / 150 wpm, ceil, return `"X min read"` / `"X mins read"`

### 3. `apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/ExploreScreen.tsx` — full rewrite
**Imports to add:** `useAuth`, `PostCard`, `PostCardSkeleton`, `FireIcon`, `BadgeCheckIcon`, `mobileApi` methods, `extractPlainText`, `extractBannerImage`, `resolveCount`, `calculateReadingTime`, `useBookmarkMutation`, `useMutation`, `socialApi`, `queryClient`, `Image`

**New data fetching:**
- `hottestQuery` — `useQuery` calling `mobileApi.getHottestMonthly(5, user?.id)`, staleTime 5 min
- Keep existing `usersQuery` and `journalsQuery` for search

**New state:**
- Existing: `query`, `activeTab`
- Derive: `isSearching = normalizedQuery.length >= 2`

**Layout — two modes:**

**A) Default mode (`!isSearching`)**
```
SafeAreaView
  Header ("Explore" + SearchInput)
  ScrollView
    Hero section
      PostCard for #1 post with "#1 Hottest" badge overlay (FireIcon + label)
    Runners section
      SectionTitle "Top This Month"
      RunnerRow × 4 (#2–#5): rank | thumbnail | title + author | hot score pill
    Loading: 3x PostCardSkeleton
    Empty: EmptyState "No hot posts yet this month"
```

**B) Search mode (`isSearching`)**
```
SafeAreaView
  Header ("Explore" + SearchInput)
  Tab chips (All / Users / Posts)
  FlatList
    Users section (same as current, with Avatar rows)
    Posts section — upgrade from text rows to PostCard components
    Empty state
```

**Runner row component** (inline, not a separate file):
- `View` row: rank number (styled in amber for #2–3), post thumbnail (60×60 rounded), title (2 lines), author name, hot score pill with FireIcon
- Pressable → navigates to PostDetail

**Hero badge overlay:**
- Absolute-positioned View on top-left of PostCard
- FireIcon + "#1 Hottest" text
- Semi-transparent dark background with amber border, rounded

**Interaction handlers:**
- Reactions, bookmarks, comments, reposts — same pattern as HomeFeedScreen
- Author press → VisitProfile or Profile tab

## Reusable pieces (no new files needed)
- `PostCard` — already supports `bannerImage`, `readingTime`, `postType`, `bannerContent`
- `PostCardSkeleton` — loading state
- `FireIcon` — already exported from icons
- `Avatar`, `Chip`, `SearchInput`, `EmptyState` — already imported
- `extractPlainText`, `extractBannerImage`, `resolveCount` — from journalHelpers
- `useBookmarkMutation` — from hooks
- Server endpoint `GET /journals/hottest-monthly` — already exists

## Files NOT changed
- No new component files — everything is inline in ExploreScreen or uses existing components
- No navigation changes — ExploreScreen route already exists
- No server changes — endpoint already exists

## Verification
- ExploreScreen shows hottest posts on load (hero + runners)
- Tapping a hottest post navigates to PostDetail
- Typing 2+ chars switches to search mode with tab filters
- Search results show PostCard for posts, Avatar rows for users
- Clearing search returns to hottest view
- Loading skeletons display while hottest data loads
- Empty state shows when no hottest posts exist
- Reactions/bookmarks work on explore PostCards
- No new TypeScript errors

        keyExtractor={item => item.id}
        renderItem={({item}) => {
          const preview = extractPlainText(item.content).slice(0, 180);
          const likeCount = resolveCount(
            item.like_count,
            item.likes_count,
          );
          const commentCount = resolveCount(
            item.comment_count,
            item.comments_count,
          );
          const bookmarkCount = resolveCount(item.bookmark_count);
          const bannerImage = extractBannerImage(
            item.content,
            (item as any).images,
          );

          const receiverId = item.user_id ?? item.users?.id ?? '';

          const handleAuthorPress = () => {
            const authorId = item.users?.id ?? item.user_id;
            if (!authorId) return;
            if (authorId === user?.id) {
              navigation.navigate('Main' as any, {screen: 'Profile'});
            } else {
              navigation.navigate('VisitProfile', {
                userId: authorId,
                username: item.users?.username ?? undefined,
              });
            }
          };

          const isRepost = !!item.is_repost;
          const repostSource = isRepost ? item.repost_source : null;
          const repostSourcePreview = repostSource?.content
            ? extractPlainText(repostSource.content).slice(0, 180)
            : '';

          return (
            <PostCard
              title={item.title || 'Untitled Post'}
              bodyPreview={preview || 'No preview text available.'}
              authorName={item.users?.name || 'Unknown author'}
              authorAvatar={item.users?.image_url}
              authorBadge={item.users?.badge as 'legend' | 'og' | undefined}
              bannerImage={bannerImage}
              postType={item.post_type}
              likeCount={likeCount}
              commentCount={commentCount}
              bookmarkCount={bookmarkCount}
              userReaction={item.user_reaction}
              isBookmarked={!!item.has_bookmarked}
              onReact={type => handleReact(item.id, receiverId, type)}
              onComment={() => handleComment(item.id, receiverId, commentCount)}
              onBookmark={() => handleBookmark(item.id)}
              onRepost={() => handleRepost(item.id, item.title ?? undefined, item.users?.name ?? undefined, item.users?.image_url ?? undefined)}
              onPress={() =>
                navigation.navigate('PostDetail', {journalId: item.id})
              }
              onAuthorPress={handleAuthorPress}
              isRepost={isRepost}
              repostCaption={item.repost_caption}
              repostSourceTitle={repostSource?.title}
              repostSourcePreview={repostSourcePreview}
              repostSourceAuthorName={repostSource?.users?.name}
              repostSourceAuthorAvatar={repostSource?.users?.image_url}
              onEmbeddedPress={repostSource ? () => navigation.navigate('PostDetail', {journalId: repostSource.id}) : undefined}
            />
          );
        }}
        ItemSeparatorComponent={() => <View style={styles.separator} />}
      />

      {commentModal && (
        <CommentModal
          visible
          postId={commentModal.postId}
          receiverId={commentModal.receiverId}
          commentCount={commentModal.commentCount}
          onClose={() => setCommentModal(null)}
        />
      )}

      {repostModal && (
        <RepostModal
          visible
          sourceJournalId={repostModal.journalId}
          title={repostModal.title}
          authorName={repostModal.authorName}
          authorAvatar={repostModal.authorAvatar}
          onClose={() => setRepostModal(null)}
        />
      )}

# Improve ExploreScreen to match webapp

## Context
The mobile ExploreScreen is currently a bare search-only page — it shows nothing until the user types 2+ characters, then displays simple text rows for results. The webapp's Explore page is much richer: it displays a **#1 Hottest post hero card**, **runners-up (#2–#5)**, and the same search functionality. This plan brings the mobile Explore page in line with the webapp.

## What changes

### Default view (before searching)
Instead of an empty state, show **monthly hottest posts**:
1. **Hero card** — The #1 hottest post as a full-width `PostCard` with a "#1 Hottest" badge (FireIcon + text overlay on top-left)
2. **Runners-up list** — Posts #2–#5 as compact ranked rows (rank number, title, author avatar+name, hot score pill)

### Search mode (when user types 2+ chars)
Keep the existing search behavior (users + posts tabs) but upgrade post results to use `PostCard` instead of plain text rows.

### New API method
Add `getHottestMonthly()` to `mobileApi.ts` — calls the existing server endpoint `GET /journals/hottest-monthly`.

### New utility
Add `calculateReadingTime()` to `journalHelpers.ts` — matches the webapp logic (150 wpm, returns "X min read").

## Files to edit

### 1. `apps/mobile/src/lib/api/mobileApi.ts`
- Add method:
  ```ts
  async getHottestMonthly(limit = 5, userId?: string): Promise<CursorPage<JournalItem>>
  ```
  Calls `GET /journals/hottest-monthly?limit={limit}&userId={userId}`

### 2. `apps/mobile/src/lib/utils/journalHelpers.ts`
- Add function:
  ```ts
  export const calculateReadingTime = (text: string): string => { ... }
  ```
  Logic: word count / 150 wpm, ceil, return `"X min read"` / `"X mins read"`

### 3. `apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/ExploreScreen.tsx` — full rewrite
**Imports to add:** `useAuth`, `PostCard`, `PostCardSkeleton`, `FireIcon`, `BadgeCheckIcon`, `mobileApi` methods, `extractPlainText`, `extractBannerImage`, `resolveCount`, `calculateReadingTime`, `useBookmarkMutation`, `useMutation`, `socialApi`, `queryClient`, `Image`

**New data fetching:**
- `hottestQuery` — `useQuery` calling `mobileApi.getHottestMonthly(5, user?.id)`, staleTime 5 min
- Keep existing `usersQuery` and `journalsQuery` for search

**New state:**
- Existing: `query`, `activeTab`
- Derive: `isSearching = normalizedQuery.length >= 2`

**Layout — two modes:**

**A) Default mode (`!isSearching`)**
```
SafeAreaView
  Header ("Explore" + SearchInput)
  ScrollView
    Hero section
      PostCard for #1 post with "#1 Hottest" badge overlay (FireIcon + label)
    Runners section
      SectionTitle "Top This Month"
      RunnerRow × 4 (#2–#5): rank | thumbnail | title + author | hot score pill
    Loading: 3x PostCardSkeleton
    Empty: EmptyState "No hot posts yet this month"
```

**B) Search mode (`isSearching`)**
```
SafeAreaView
  Header ("Explore" + SearchInput)
  Tab chips (All / Users / Posts)
  FlatList
    Users section (same as current, with Avatar rows)
    Posts section — upgrade from text rows to PostCard components
    Empty state
```

**Runner row component** (inline, not a separate file):
- `View` row: rank number (styled in amber for #2–3), post thumbnail (60×60 rounded), title (2 lines), author name, hot score pill with FireIcon
- Pressable → navigates to PostDetail

**Hero badge overlay:**
- Absolute-positioned View on top-left of PostCard
- FireIcon + "#1 Hottest" text
- Semi-transparent dark background with amber border, rounded

**Interaction handlers:**
- Reactions, bookmarks, comments, reposts — same pattern as HomeFeedScreen
- Author press → VisitProfile or Profile tab

## Reusable pieces (no new files needed)
- `PostCard` — already supports `bannerImage`, `readingTime`, `postType`, `bannerContent`
- `PostCardSkeleton` — loading state
- `FireIcon` — already exported from icons
- `Avatar`, `Chip`, `SearchInput`, `EmptyState` — already imported

The All / Users / Posts filter chips on the Explore screen look oversized. The shared `Chip` component uses `paddingHorizontal: 16` and `paddingVertical: 8` — too chunky for tab-style filter chips. ## Approach
Add an optional `size` prop to the `Chip` component (`'sm' | 'md'`, default `'md'`). Use `size="sm"` in `ExploreScreen`. This keeps existing usages unchanged.

- Add `size?: 'sm' | 'md'` to `ChipProps` (default `'md'`)
- `sm`: `paddingHorizontal: 10`, `paddingVertical: 4`, `fontSize: 12`
- `md`: current values (`paddingHorizontal: 16`, `paddingVertical: 8`, `fontSize: 13`)

### 2. `apps/mobile/src/screens/Home/ExploreScreen.tsx`
- Pass `size="sm"` to each `<Chip>` in the search tab row (line ~314)

## Verification
- Explore tab chips render smaller and more proportionate
- HomeFeedScreen chips unchanged (still use default `md`)
- No TypeScript errors
